_comment: Synthetic hand-labeled corpus for the M4.0 calibration harness (ADR-0038). ZERO API calls produced this — every finding and every label is authored, not captured. It exercises severity grouping (critical/elevated/routine cohorts), the MAX rule (crit-max cites a critical AND a routine ADR → critical), the no-ADR default (route-s3 cites a manifest, not an ADR → routine), and cosmetic exclusion (cosmetic-note cites only the cosmetic ADR → counted in sampleSize, absent from every channel). The corpus is deliberately small, so every channel stays closed: a small corpus opens nothing, and that is the correct, published outcome. The curve's opening path is proven separately with explicit arrays in the unit tests.

findings.claim: The signing key is read into process memory in the build cache path, against the HSM-only decision.

citations.quote: no code path reads the key material into process memory

findings.consequence: A build-host compromise could exfiltrate a key the decision says it can never hold.

findings.claim: A second service exports the private key for a local signing shortcut.

citations.quote: generated inside the HSM and never exported

findings.consequence: The key leaves the module, which is the exact failure this decision exists to prevent.

findings.claim: The rotation script rewrites both the HSM ceremony doc and the UTC storage note, touching two decisions at once.

citations.quote: Key rotation is an HSM ceremony, not a config change

findings.consequence: A change spanning a critical and a routine decision inherits the critical consequence (the MAX rule).

findings.claim: The billing service forwards trace ids but the notifier drops the header.

citations.quote: forwards it on every downstream call

findings.consequence: Requests through the notifier lose correlation, degrading every downstream investigation.

findings.claim: The edge assigns a fresh trace id even when one is present.

citations.quote:
assigned a fresh id at
the edge, never in the interior

findings.consequence: A pre-existing trace id would be discarded, breaking cross-boundary correlation.

findings.claim: A migration writes local time into one legacy column.
findings.consequence: One column drifts from the UTC storage rule, complicating cross-region comparison.

findings.claim: A report renders timestamps without applying the viewer zone.
findings.consequence: Render-time localization is skipped in one view.

findings.claim: A retry-backoff policy is implemented in code with no ADR recording the decision.
findings.consequence: An unrecorded operational decision cites a manifest, not an ADR, so it takes the routine default.

findings.claim: One service emits uppercase WARN against the lowercase level-name note.

citations.quote: Structured log level names are lowercase

findings.consequence: A cosmetic-only finding: counted in the corpus, but it can never open a channel.
